news_items.headline: Google to Pay SpaceX Nearly $1 Billion a Month in Cloud-Computing Deal
news_items.summary: Google has agreed to rent data-center capacity from SpaceX, expanding the rocket company’s artificial-intelligence business ahead of its initial public offering. Google will pay SpaceX $920 million a month from October 2026 to June 2029 in a deal that includes the computing capacity of at least 110,000 Nvidia chips, according to a SpaceX securities filing on Friday. The deal will ramp up over summer, and Google has the right to cancel the agreement in October if SpaceX doesn’t provide the promised chips, SpaceX said.
